name: Peccei-Quinn axion
summary: A new spontaneously broken global U(1)_PQ chiral symmetry, anomalous under QCD, dynamically relaxes the strong-CP angle to zero. The pseudo-Goldstone boson of the broken symmetry is the axion, with mass m_a ~ Lambda_QCD^2 / f_a set by the symmetry-breaking scale. Cold-axion populations from the misalignment mechanism can also constitute the dark matter.

addresses_puzzles.note: Dynamical relaxation of theta_eff to zero is the textbook solution; the prediction is independent of UV physics once the PQ symmetry exists.
Misalignment mechanism produces cold axion DM with relic density set by f_a and theta_initial; for f_a in 10^11 - 10^12 GeV the axion can saturate Omega_DM.

touches_benchmarks.note: Axion-induced oscillating EDM at frequency m_a is a target for CASPEr-type experiments; magnitude set by axion-gluon coupling.
Axion-photon conversion in microwave cavity; ADMX has reached DFSZ sensitivity over part of the QCD-axion mass range.

new_fields: PQ-charged heavy fermion (KSVZ) or extra Higgs doublet (DFSZ)
introduces.note: f_a is the dominant new parameter; the axion mass is then derived. KSVZ adds one heavy-fermion mass; DFSZ adds the second Higgs sector with its own potential, modestly increasing the count.

history: Peccei-Quinn (1977); axion identified by Wilczek (1978) and Weinberg (1978). KSVZ (1979) and DFSZ (1980, 1981) variants. Astrophysical constraints (SN1987A, horizontal branch stars) excluded f_a < ~10^8 GeV. ADMX has been searching the QCD-axion window since the 2000s.

references.citation: Peccei and Quinn, 'CP conservation in the presence of pseudoparticles', PRL 38, 1440 (1977)
Wilczek, 'Problem of strong P and T invariance in the presence of instantons', PRL 40, 279 (1978)
